title: celtis occidentalis var. grandidentata
intro: Celtis occidentalis var. grandidentata, also known as the eastern hackberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to eastern North America. Its bark is rich in tannins and has been used in traditional medicine for its astringent and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The plant has various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, antimicrobial properties, and antioxidant activity, which may help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. The herbal preparations of celtis occidentalis var. grandidentata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
